name: Fallow: severity + baseline/ratchet plan
description: Decide whether rules.unused-dependencies should be warn vs error; decide whether/when to introduce baselines (dead-code/dupes/health) and ratchet over time; document intended steady-state gate.

name: Structured instructions_and_notes in schema
description: Zod schema plus prompts and helper so Claude returns instructions_and_notes as structured output.
result: Added structured instructionsAndNotes to recipe URL extraction schema, prompt, markdown formatter, and query draft wiring.

name: Fallow: deps hygiene (unused + test-only deps)
description: Decide and implement: remove @tanstack/react-router-ssr-query if truly unused; move @cloudflare/vite-plugin, @tailwindcss/vite, drizzle-kit to devDependencies if safe for our deploy/install story. Validate via bun run build/typecheck/test.

name: Demo project: Homelab spring cleaning
description: Synthetic dex demo — greg profile parent task

name: Recipe URL: compile additional golden samples
description:
Build a small, representative corpus of recipe URL golden samples before adding more fixtures/harness assertions. Candidate samples:

- The Woks of Life 1-2-3-4-5 Ribs, https://thewoksoflife.com/1-2-3-4-5-ribs/. Useful for numbered title, ratio-based sauce ingredients, Chinese pantry terms, ingredient prep notes, and notes embedded in article prose.
- AllRecipes Authentic Louisiana Red Beans and Rice, https://www.allrecipes.com/recipe/58211/authentic-louisiana-red-beans-and-rice/. Useful as another AllRecipes page with a longer ingredient list, regional dish naming, legumes/rice/sausage aromatics, and likely richer cook-time/instruction structure.
- Damn Delicious Red Beans and Rice, https://damndelicious.net/2019/04/15/red-beans-and-rice/. Useful for rendered ingredient formatting, prep-note boundaries, alternatives (small red beans or kidney beans), salt/pepper splitting, optional instructions/garnishes, and heavy page noise around the recipe card.

Important: live pages are only for discovery/validation. Real golden samples must be checked-in static HTML/pageDigest fixtures plus expected assertions, not live network fetches. For each sample, capture why it is useful, expected extraction stress cases, and whether it should become a checked-in fixture.
result: Compiled recipe URL golden sample candidates in src/lib/ai/recipe-from-url/fixtures/golden-samples.md, including static-fixture rules and stress cases for AllRecipes Tater Tot Casserole, Woks of Life 1-2-3-4-5 Ribs, AllRecipes Louisiana Red Beans and Rice, and Damn Delicious Red Beans and Rice.

name: Ingredient goldens: local-only runner command
description:
Provide a single local command (Bun script) to run all ingredient goldens.

Constraints:
- Not intended for CI (it costs money)

Done when:
- There is a documented local command that runs all ingredient goldens end-to-end
- It performs no Spoonacular calls (only uses stored fixtures + Claude calls)
result:
Documented the single local runner command for ingredient goldens:
- bun run spoonacular:ingredient:pick:golden (Claude calls only; no Spoonacular)
Also documented snapshot generation command:
- bun run spoonacular:ingredient:snapshot (hits Spoonacular to refresh fixtures)
Docs live in src/lib/ai/spoonacular/fixtures/golden/README.md

name: sample
description: Scratch and demo dex tasks. Safe to ignore for real work.

name: Recipe URL: golden HTML + Claude I/O harness
description: Isolate recipe-from-URL from live fetch. Static pageDigest fixture, AllRecipes golden URL, structured Claude output, script or test, optional golden JSON and evaluator LLM.
result: Completed recipe URL golden fixture and Claude I/O harness work: structured instructionsAndNotes schema/prompt/UI wiring, AllRecipes 222037 fixture and expected assertions, and passing Bun golden script.

name: Milestone 4 (optional): Brewfile + brew bundle
description: Move package lists to Brewfile(s) and use brew bundle; keep wrapper for group prompts.

name: Fallow: verify CI audit behavior
description: Confirm CI job uses fallow-rs/fallow@v2 command=audit with fetch-depth=0; ensure it behaves as new-only gate (does not fail PRs for inherited repo debt). Optionally adjust workflow inputs (format/comment/annotations) as desired.

name: Lock acceptance before first LLM run
description: Agree with Greg on expected recipe fields and where golden expected output will live.
result: Locked golden acceptance in allrecipes-222037.expected.json and verified the fixture harness shape.

name: App feature: paste full recipe + URL (alternate query tab)
description:
Goal: add an alternate mode in the real app where a user pastes an entire recipe plus the source URL, and we run the same AI extraction flow.

Requirements:
- UI: new tab/option on the main query surface
- Input: user provides (a) the full recipe text and (b) the URL (used to dedupe / prevent rescans)
- Server: reuse the same extraction pipeline as URL mode, but pageDigest comes from pasted text (normalized/truncated similarly)
- Safety: still enforce URL normalization/dedupe checks; no unauthenticated access

Done when:
- User can choose URL mode or Paste mode
- Paste mode submits {url, pastedText} and produces the same RecipeQueryResult shape
- Existing URL mode behavior unchanged
- Basic fixture-based test/harness exists for pasted text normalization + extraction prompt input (no live network needed)

name: Fallow: adopt + manage findings
description:
Current state (already done)
- fallow installed (devDependency): fallow@2.66.1
- package.json: trustedDependencies includes fallow
- .fallowrc.json exists (strict JSON)
- .gitignore includes .fallow/
- CI: .github/workflows/continuous-integration.yaml has job fallow using fallow-rs/fallow@v2 with command=audit and checkout fetch-depth=0

Observed findings (from bun run fallow)
- Dead code: 31 issues (26 unused exports, 1 unused type, 1 unused dependency, 3 test-only production deps)
- Dupes: 10 clone groups (~8.3% dup rate)
- Health: 32 above threshold
- Deps detail (from fallow dead-code --unused-deps):
  - Unused dependency: @tanstack/react-router-ssr-query
  - Test-only production deps (candidate move to devDependencies): @cloudflare/vite-plugin, @tailwindcss/vite, drizzle-kit

Scope / intent
- Primary: verify CI audit behavior and fix safe deps hygiene.
- Do NOT: large refactors for dupes/health yet (defer unless explicitly selected).
- Severity: decide whether unused-dependencies should stay warn vs become error after signal is trusted.

Commands (local)
- bun run fallow
- bunx fallow dead-code --unused-deps
- bun run build
- bun run typecheck
- bun run test

Acceptance criteria
- CI fallow job runs and gates via fallow audit as intended (new-only by default).
- Dependency decisions are made and validated via build/typecheck/test.
- If removing/moving deps: bun run build + typecheck + test still pass.

name: Fallow: duplication baseline + 2 clone families
description: 1) Save dupes baseline + wire audit.dupesBaseline. 2) Extract shared helpers in routes (ingredient + recipe.index). 3) Extract shared helpers in scripts (recipe-url-golden + spoonacular-ingredient-pick-golden).

name: Script/prod parity audit: unify helpers + assurance
description:
Goal: ensure script harnesses and production code paths share the same helpers and that we have confidence prod behavior matches what scripts test.

Scope:
- Inventory helper functions used by scripts (URL fetch/digest, normalization, schema validation, prompt assembly)
- Compare to production entrypoints and ensure a single source of truth (no drift)
- Add lightweight verification harnesses that run without live network where possible (fixtures-based)

Done when:
- Documented map of script->prod helper usage
- Any duplicated logic is refactored to shared helpers (or explicitly justified)
- At least one fixture-based check validates prod helpers produce the same intermediate artifacts as scripts (e.g. pageDigest normalization)

name: Harness script: fixture to Claude to checks
description: Bun script or test loads fixture, calls extractRecipeWithClaudeFromPageDigest, asserts or uses comparison LLM.
result: Added Bun golden harness script that loads the fixture, calls extractRecipeWithClaudeFromPageDigest, and asserts the recipe contract.

name: Ingredient goldens: Claude candidate-pick harness
description:
Add a golden harness that feeds stored Spoonacular candidates into Claude (same as prod) and evaluates whether the chosen ingredient is reasonable.

Constraints:
- We cannot fix Spoonacular; the evaluation is whether Claude’s selection from Spoonacular’s candidate list is acceptable

Done when:
- Harness runs through all N fixtures
- For each fixture we assert: picked vs no_match expectations, and if picked, ingredientId is within an allowed set for that query
- No Spoonacular network calls during harness runs
result:
Added Spoonacular ingredient pick golden harness (Claude):
- Snapshot fixtures (*.spoonacular.json) + expectations (*.expected.json)
- New script: bun run spoonacular:ingredient:pick:golden
- Asserts Claude's pick is in an acceptable ID allowlist per ingredient
- No Spoonacular calls during harness runs

Initial 4 cases pass locally.

name: Milestone 3: refactor structure + DRY helpers
description: Centralize install helpers; reduce repetition; keep argument handling/help in sync with available tasks.
result: Deduplicated task handling: introduced TASKS registry, generated usage output, unified run_task dispatcher, and made install_everything iterate tasks.

name: Investigation: batch ingredient evaluation to reduce LLM cost
description:
Goal: investigate whether we can have the LLM evaluate multiple desired ingredients against Spoonacular candidate lists in a single call to reduce per-ingredient cost.

Questions to answer:
- Prompt/schema design to handle N independent picks reliably
- Failure modes (one bad item poisoning whole batch)
- Token/cost tradeoffs vs per-item calls
- How to record goldens for batched mode (static Spoonacular fixtures + expected picks)

Done when:
- A short design doc (in-repo markdown) proposes an approach, constraints, and go/no-go criteria
- A small prototype script can run batched picks on existing fixtures and report per-item results

name: brews.sh cleanup
description: Cleanup/refactor scripts/brews.sh for consistency, safety, and maintainability.

name: Ingredient goldens: derive candidates from recipe goldens
description:
While building the ingredient golden set, pull good ingredient-query candidates from existing recipe golden fixtures (mix easy/medium/hard).

Done when:
- We’ve reviewed current recipe golden fixtures and selected candidate ingredient names
- The ingredient golden list incorporates these where they improve coverage (ambiguity, synonyms, multi-word, branded, etc.)
result:
Reviewed existing recipe URL golden fixtures and derived 6 additional candidate ingredient queries (to grow toward 10 total) with suggested spoonacularQuery strings.
Recorded in: src/lib/ai/spoonacular/fixtures/golden/candidates-from-recipe-goldens.md

name: Ingredient goldens: wrap-up + expand to 10
description:
Goal: finish and harden the ingredient testing/golden workflow.

Scope:
- Expand ingredient goldens to 10 total (or document why not)
- Snapshot Spoonacular once per missing fixture (no re-fetching existing fixtures)
- Define/maintain expected allowlists for Claude pick per ingredient
- Ensure commands are documented and repeatable locally

Done when:
- ingredient-queries.json has the final selected set (<=10)
- For each entry: corresponding *.spoonacular.json + *.expected.json exists
- bun run spoonacular:ingredient:pick:golden passes for all
- README documents snapshot vs pick runs and cost implications

name: Backups: verify restic snapshots
description: Dry-run restore of last weekly snapshot

description: Umbrella for grynthia.cat repo work. Use child tasks for concrete efforts.
result:
Marking this umbrella as complete per request.

What’s complete:

Note:

name: Ingredient goldens: choose fixture set
description:
Goal: match recipe golden case count with ingredient queries (easy/medium/hard).

Done when:
- A list of N ingredient queries exists (N = current recipe golden cases)
- Each query is tagged easy/medium/hard
- Each query has a brief note explaining why it’s a good test
result:
Chose initial ingredient golden query set matching current recipe golden count (N=4) and generated Spoonacular snapshot fixtures:
- Olive Oil
- Pork Belly (adjusted from 'Pork Belly with Skin On' due to zero Spoonacular search results)
- Gochugaru
- Taro Leaves

All snapshots written as static *.spoonacular.json fixtures under src/lib/ai/spoonacular/fixtures/golden/.

name: Milestone 1: shell + paths consistent
description: Decide Bash vs POSIX sh; fix redirection portability; use /Users/greg.linscheid, avoid hardcoded /Users/...
result: Set brews.sh to bash, fixed portable brew check redirection, and removed hardcoded home path in .zprofile writes.

name: Milestone 2: remove eval + add safety/guards
description: Replace eval-based command execution; add strict mode and sensible traps; guard steps that assume tools (pip3/pipx/etc).
result: Removed eval-based installs by switching prompt helper to execute command+args; added strict bash mode; added shell-only helper for pipelines/&&; added python3 guard for copyparty install.

name: Check in golden pageDigest fixture
description: Commit static HTML slice matching fetchRecipePageDigest truncation plus canonical URL for AllRecipes recipe 222037.
result: Checked in static AllRecipes 222037 HTML digest fixture plus expected structured assertions.

name: Ingredient goldens: snapshot Spoonacular search + info
description:
Add a script that, for each chosen ingredient query, captures and writes static fixtures:
- Spoonacular ingredient search results list (id, name, image)
- The ingredient information response for the chosen ingredient (includes nutrition)

Constraints:
- This step is allowed to hit Spoonacular once per query (during snapshot generation)
- Outputs are static JSON committed to repo

Done when:
- Running the snapshot script generates fixtures for all N queries
result:
Added Spoonacular ingredient snapshot workflow:
- New fixture dir: src/lib/ai/spoonacular/fixtures/golden/
- ingredient-queries.json seeds initial 4 queries (matches current recipe golden count)
- bun script spoonacular:ingredient:snapshot fetches Spoonacular search + ingredient information (100g) and writes static *.spoonacular.json fixtures
- No AI/Claude calls in snapshot step
